title: The Stay at the Grand Hotel Frankfurter Hof
subtitle: Frankfurt am Main, 1911

chapters.title: A Color Illustration
chapters.body: The Grand Hotel Frankfurter Hof opened in 1876. By 1911, when this postcard was printed, it was one of the most prestigious establishments in Germany — a lodging for emperors, politicians, and celebrities of the nascent twentieth century. The hotel was among the first to use electric lighting and had Frankfurt's first telephone line.

chapters.title: The Street in 1911
chapters.body: A Tin Lizzie (Ford Model T), another car of an unidentified model, a horse-drawn carriage, and many passersby in elegant dress. A scene that could easily be mistaken for Paris or New York.

chapters.title: The Connection to the Present
chapters.body: Without flowers adorning the façade, the building seems to have lost its color.
chapters.annotation: Epizentrum, CC BY-SA 3.0, via Wikimedia Commons

chapters.title: Where the Person Meets the Object
chapters.body: We stayed here on May 28 and 29, 1911. We had the first-floor apartment, comprising the sitting room, 3 bedrooms and the bathroom, plus a separate room for Marta and Manuela. Price: 84 marks; without meals, 59 marks.
